THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter launched a new Parks Committee with plans for a major Bushka Park playground overhaul, while the Wausau School Board opened contract talks with its teachers union for the coming school year.

  • Kronenwetter's new Parks Committee elected Erin Mishka as chair and Dan Lesniac as vice chair at its first-ever meeting June 29, immediately turning to a $150,000–$200,000 playground replacement at Bushka Park.
  • The committee scheduled a public parks tour for July 8 at 4 p.m. at the municipal center, with July 9 as a rain date, and set a July 17 movie-under-the-stars fundraiser for the playground project.
  • The Wausau Board of Education met July 1 to exchange initial written contract proposals with the Wausau Education Association for the 2026-27 school year.
  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee is set to vote on revisions to its Chapter 382 nuisances ordinance and consider traffic control measures on County Road XX and Terrebonne Drive at its July 6 meeting.

Coming up: Monday brings a full slate including Wausau's Parks & Recreation Committee at 5:15 p.m., the Village of Weston's Board of Trustees and Community Life & Public Safety Committee, and Kronenwetter's CLIPP meeting at 6 p.m. Later in the week, Marathon County's Infrastructure Committee meets Thursday and the City of Wausau's Infrastructure & Facilities and Sustainability, Energy & Environment committees both convene Thursday evening.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
